lenses.description: The longest of the seven Cinema Vista primes in this set and the fast short-telephoto of the line, notable as a T1.5 lens at this focal length. It brings strong subject isolation and compression with the series' sharp, high-resolution, neutral rendering across full-frame and larger sensors. Smooth bokeh from the nine-blade iris, near-zero breathing and matched gearing keep it interchangeable with the shorter primes. It holds the clean Vista baseline look.

The widest prime in the Cinema Vista set and a genuine engineering feat: a fast T1.5 ultra-wide that still throws an image circle large enough to cover full-frame, VistaVision and larger sensors. It renders with the clean, neutral, high-resolution character that defines the standard Vista line, the reference look from which the Vista-C and Vista-P variants were derived. Distortion and chromatic aberration are held low for a lens this wide, with a rounded nine-blade iris and roughly 300 degrees of focus rotation. Of the set it shows marginally more focus breathing than the longer primes, though the effect stays slight.

A wide-angle Cinema Vista that carries the line's purpose-built, neutral rendering into interiors and landscapes. It resolves cleanly across a full-frame and VistaVision image circle with low distortion and well-controlled chromatic aberration, colour-matched to the rest of the set. Near-zero focus breathing, a rounded iris and a shared front diameter and gear position make it a direct swap with its neighbours. This is the clean baseline optic, not the vintage-flavoured Vista-C or the swirl-heavy Vista-P.

A wide-normal and one of the workhorse focal lengths of the Cinema Vista set, suited to establishing shots and moving work. It keeps the series' sharp, high-resolution, low-distortion rendering across large-format coverage, colour-matched for seamless cutting with the other primes. Virtually no focus breathing and identical mechanics support fast lens changes on set. It represents the clean, neutral Vista baseline behind the character variants.

A storytelling wide-normal at the heart of the Cinema Vista set, a natural choice for dialogue and coverage. Purpose-built for cinema rather than rehoused, it delivers tack-sharp resolution wide open at T1.5 with low distortion and neutral colour across full-frame and larger sensors. Near-zero breathing, a nine-blade round iris and matched gearing keep it consistent with the rest of the range. It is the clean reference look behind the Vista-C and Vista-P character variants.

The normal lens of the Cinema Vista set and a natural anchor for the range. It pairs a sharp, high-resolution centre with even, neutral rendering across a 46.7mm image circle, holding the line's low distortion and controlled chromatic aberration. The nine-blade iris gives round, natural bokeh, focus breathing is virtually absent, and the barrel matches the set for quick swaps. This is the clean baseline Vista optic.

The classic portrait focal length of the Cinema Vista set, delivering flattering compression with the line's clean, modern rendering. Sharp and high-resolution wide open at T1.5, it isolates the subject against smooth, natural bokeh from the rounded nine-blade iris, with neutral colour and low aberration across full-frame coverage. Focus breathing is virtually absent and the mechanics match the rest of the set. It is the neutral baseline, without the flares of the Vista-C or the swirl of the Vista-P.

lenses.description: A short-telephoto full-frame prime in Tokina's Vista-C line, unusually fast for its reach at T1.5. It keeps the set's vintage character, lower contrast, gentle distortion and warm flares, alongside high resolution and near-zero focus breathing, and the nine-blade curved iris renders smooth, round highlights for strong subject isolation. Its image circle covers large-format sensors. The 114mm front, long focus throw and common gear positions keep it consistent with the rest of the set.

It carries the redesigned Vista-C optics, softened contrast and edges with warm red and blue flares, while holding resolution and keeping focus breathing near zero across long pulls. The curved nine-blade iris renders round, smooth bokeh, and coverage extends to large-format sensors. It shares the 114mm front, focus rotation and gear positions common to the whole line.

lenses.description: The longest lens in Tokina's Vista-C set, a full-frame telephoto at T1.9, the one focal length that steps back from the line's T1.5 core. It matches the vintage Vista-C rendering, lowered contrast, soft edges and warm flares, with high resolution and near-zero breathing for controlled long-distance pulls. The nine-blade curved iris keeps out-of-focus highlights round for portraits and isolation, and its image circle covers large-format sensors. The 114mm front, long focus throw and shared gear positions let it swap cleanly with the rest of the set.

lenses.description: The widest lens in Tokina's Vista-C set, a full-frame ultra-wide that carries the line's deliberately vintage character at a fast T1.5. Where the standard Vista primes chase a clean, uniform look, the Vista-C redesign lowers contrast, introduces subtle spherical distortion and softens the edges, trading the usual green flares for warmer red and blue ones. Its image circle covers full frame comfortably, though at this wide focal length it does not fully reach the very largest formats such as ALEXA 65 Open Gate. The curved nine-blade iris keeps out-of-focus highlights round, and the shared 114mm front, long focus throw and common gear positions let it swap quickly with the rest of the set.

The nine-blade curved iris gives round, smooth out-of-focus areas, and coverage extends past full frame. Like the rest of the set it uses the 114mm front, roughly 300 degrees of focus rotation and matched gear positions for fast, consistent lens changes.

As one of the wider focal lengths its image circle covers full frame but not the largest open-gate formats, and the nine-blade iris keeps bokeh round. It shares the 114mm front, focus throw and gear positions common to the whole set.

The nine-blade curved iris gives round, smooth out-of-focus rendering, and coverage extends well past full frame to large-format sensors. It shares the 114mm front, long focus throw and common gear positions that let the whole set swap quickly.

Coverage runs beyond full frame to large formats. It shares the 114mm front, roughly 300 degrees of focus rotation and matched gear positions with the rest of the line for fast changes.

lenses.description: A telephoto portrait lens that brings stronger compression to the Vista-P line. The deliberate spherical character and reduced contrast wrap the out-of-focus areas around a crisp centre, giving close and medium portraits a flattering, organic separation. It holds the series standard of minimal breathing, a matched iris and a long, even focus feel for repeatable marks. The stylised fall-off is most pronounced on full-frame and larger sensors.

The Portrait re-engineering keeps a sharp, high-resolution centre while the background falls off with the line's soft, swirling, lower-contrast rendering. Near-zero breathing and matched mechanics make it interchangeable with the rest of the set on the day. Its character is strongest wide open across full-frame coverage.

lenses.description: The longest lens in the Vista-P set and the one telephoto that opens a touch slower than the rest of the primes, developed to give large-format work a fast super-telephoto reach. It delivers dramatic compression and subject isolation with a sharp centre and the line's soft, spherically-influenced, lower-contrast fall-off in the background. Internal focusing keeps its length fixed and breathing near zero, and it carries the same matched front and gearing as the shorter primes despite its size. The character suits stylised portrait and specialty telephoto work on full-frame and larger sensors.

lenses.description: The widest Vista-P prime, a factory re-engineering of the standard Vista in which elements are repositioned throughout the design to introduce deliberate spherical aberration and reduced contrast. At this wide angle the Petzval-like swirl and field character sit most visibly toward the edges of the full-frame image, while the centre keeps the crisp, high-resolution rendering of the base Vista optics. It retains the series baseline of near-zero focus breathing, a rounded iris and a matched front for quick swaps. The stylised edge effect is strongest across full-frame coverage and relaxes on Super 35.

The repositioned elements bring a gentle spherical fall-off and swirl to the outer frame while the centre stays sharp, giving wide interiors and landscapes an organic, slightly vintage feel. Focus breathing is effectively absent and the barrel matches the rest of the set for gearing and front diameter. On Super 35 the edge character tightens up.

lenses.description: A short telephoto that begins the compression and subject-isolation range of the Vista-P line. The Portrait re-engineering gives it a clean centre with a gentle spherical fall-off and swirl behind the subject, lending close portraits an organic, slightly vintage separation. It shares the family's near-zero breathing, matched iris and front for quick changes. Character increases toward the edges of the full-frame image.

sources.gearPitch: Reasoning: Vista-P shares the Cinema Vista housing; 0.8 mod standard cine gears across the Vista line

lenses.description: The classic portrait focal length of the Vista-P set, where the line's intent is most at home. A sharp, high-resolution centre isolates the subject while the repositioned Vista elements render the surrounding fall-off with a soft, swirling, lower-contrast character. Near-zero focus breathing keeps the frame stable through slow pulls, and the barrel matches the rest of the set for gearing and front size. The effect is at its most expressive wide open on full-frame.
